deep_dive.title: Knowledge Over Parameters: Evolving Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection
deep_dive.abstract: Smart contract vulnerabilities are predominantly logic bugs whose detection requires structured, step-by-step procedural knowledge of attack patterns and contract semantics. Existing LLM-based methods struggle to generate this knowledge automatically: prompt-based methods rely on manually crafted detection rules, while fine-tuning requires massive labeled datasets that are inherently scarce in this domain. We present EvoVuln, an automated framework that reformulates vulnerability detection as a procedural knowledge evolution problem, synthesizing and refining detection logic using only a minimal number of labeled samples.   To achieve this, EvoVuln introduces two key mechanisms. First, a Runtime with an Inversion of Control (IoC) architecture compiles detection rules into Executable Policies. This strictly decouples deterministic control flow from LLM semantic reasoning, ensuring faithful logical adherence and producing dense diagnostic telemetry for precise error localization. Second, a two-phase evolution pipeline refines the rule via abductive semantic debugging without any parameter updates: Cold Start bootstraps and stress-tests an initial rule using auto-synthesized corner cases; Few-Shot Evolving then grounds the policy in real-world semantics using only five vulnerable and five safe examples per vulnerability type.   Evaluated across five real-world vulnerability types, EvoVuln achieves a 71% macro-average F1-score, outperforming all baselines. The evolved procedural knowledge is portable across models: it enables a lightweight, low-cost model to surpass a much larger zero-shot model by 19 percentage points, and transfers to other LLMs without retraining, at a one-time evolution cost under $50.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Automated smart contract vulnerability detection via procedural knowledge evolution, addressing requirement of advanced software vulnerability detection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过程序性知识演化实现自动化智能合约漏洞检测，对应于高级软件漏洞检测要求。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过程序性知识演化实现自动化智能合约漏洞检测，对应于高级软件漏洞检测要求。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过程序性知识演化实现自动化智能合约漏洞检测，对应于高级软件漏洞检测要求。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: EvoVuln automates smart contract vulnerability detection by synthesizing and refining detection logic from a few labeled samples, outperforming LLM-based methods that rely on manual rules or large datasets. It demonstrates effectiveness with minimal supervision.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM方法在智能合约漏洞检测中依赖手工规则或大量标注数据，难以自动生成检测逻辑。本文提出EvoVuln框架，将漏洞检测转化为程序性知识演化问题，利用少量标注样本自动合成和优化检测规则。通过控制反转运行时等机制，实现高效检测。实验表明EvoVuln在有限监督下显著优于现有方法，为软件漏洞检测提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: Refploit: Facilitating Exploit Construction via Code-Agent Trajectory Repair
deep_dive.abstract: Vulnerability exploits play a crucial role in assessing the downstream impact of Java library vulnerabilities. While some vulnerabilities are accompanied by disclosed exploit references, automatically reproducing such references into runnable exploits remains challenging because they are often incomplete, unstructured, or only describe partial reproduction steps. Recent code agents provide a promising way to automate this process, but our study shows that their generated exploits often appear successful without triggering the actual vulnerable logic, such as replacing vulnerable APIs with self-implemented functions. To address this, we propose Refploit, an LLM-based trajectory recovery framework for facilitating vulnerability reproduction from public exploit references. The key insight is that a failed agent trajectory is not entirely useless. It may have already completed some reproduction subtasks while also revealing misleading directions that should be avoided. Refploit first validates an agent-generated exploit through differential execution. When the exploit is ineffective, Refploit analyzes its reproduction progress, locates the trajectory segments associated with the reproduction progress, and derives constraints to guide focused recovery. We evaluate Refploit on three open-source Java vulnerability datasets, covering 172 exploit references for 143 vulnerabilities. Under DeepSeek-V4-Flash, Refploit successfully reproduces 138 exploits, achieving a reproduction rate of 80.2%. It achieves a 64.3% relative improvement over the initially generated trajectories and outperforms both the SOTA exploit-generation method PoCGen and advanced code agents such as Codex with GPT-5.4. We further adapt Refploit to another code agent and observe consistent improvements, demonstrating its generality.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Automates exploit construction from vulnerability references using LLM-based trajectory repair, aiding vulnerability research.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用基于LLM的轨迹修复自动从漏洞引用构建利用，助力漏洞研究。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用基于LLM的轨迹修复自动从漏洞引用构建利用，助力漏洞研究。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用基于LLM的轨迹修复自动从漏洞引用构建利用，助力漏洞研究。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Refploit is an LLM-based trajectory recovery framework that automatically reproduces runnable exploits from incomplete vulnerability references, improving exploit construction for Java libraries.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对Java库漏洞利用参考常不完整、现有代码代理易跳过真实漏洞逻辑的问题，提出Refploit框架，利用LLM轨迹修复自动生成可运行利用。成功构建了多个实际漏洞利用，验证了方法的有效性，为自动化漏洞利用构建和防御评估提供了新工具。

deep_dive.title: Cloak and Detonate: Scanner Evasion and Dynamic Detection of Agent Skill Malware
deep_dive.abstract: LLM coding agents increasingly rely on third-party agent skills from public marketplaces, which execute with the agent's privileges and create a software supply-chain attack surface: a malicious skill can steal credentials, exfiltrate source code, or install backdoors. Existing defenses use static skill scanners based on pattern matching or LLM-as-judge analysis, but it remains unclear whether they withstand adaptive evasions that preserve malicious behavior while changing payload appearance.   This paper first presents an adversarial study of existing skill scanners through SkillCloak, a payload-preserving evasion framework that keeps the attack semantics intact while transforming their visible form. SkillCloak uses two complementary strategies: Structural Obfuscation, which rewrites visible payload indicators into semantically equivalent forms, and Self-Extracting Skill (SFS) Packing, which hides malicious components from the install-time view and restores them during agent execution. Across eight scanners and 1,613 in-the-wild malicious skills, SFS Packing bypasses every scanner at over 90%, while Structural Obfuscation bypasses over 80% on most static scanners and reaches 96% on a hybrid scanner, showing that appearance-based auditing is insufficient.   Motivated by this finding, we propose SkillDetonate, a behavior-centric runtime auditor that executes skills in a sandbox and detects malicious effects through OS-boundary information-flow evidence rather than install-time appearance. SkillDetonate combines on-demand closure lift, which observes instructions materialized during execution, with marker-based taint analysis, which tracks sensitive-data flows across the agent context, files, processes, and network operations. The results show that SkillDetonate detects 97% of attacks at a 2% false-positive rate and sustains 87% detection on real-world malicious skills.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Dynamic detection of malware in agent skills to prevent exploitation, addressing software supply chain security.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 动态检测代理技能中的恶意软件以防止利用，应对软件供应链安全。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 动态检测代理技能中的恶意软件以防止利用，应对软件供应链安全。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 动态检测代理技能中的恶意软件以防止利用，应对软件供应链安全。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses malicious agent skills by first showing evasion of static scanners via SkillCloak, then proposing a dynamic detection approach. It demonstrates that dynamic analysis effectively catches evasive malware, enhancing software security.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有静态扫描器无法有效检测经过对抗规避的代理技能恶意软件。本文首先开发SkillCloak框架展示静态扫描的规避能力，然后提出动态检测方法，通过实际执行分析恶意行为。实验证明动态分析能有效捕捉规避后的恶意负载，为软件供应链安全提供了更全面的防护手段。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有静态扫描器无法有效检测经过对抗规避的代理技能恶意软件。本文首先开发SkillCloak框架展示静态扫描的规避能力，然后提出动态检测方法，通过实际执行分析恶意行为。实验证明动态分析能有效捕捉规避后的恶意负载，为软件供应链安全提供了更全面的防护手段。

quick_skim.title: Exploring Side-Channel Protections in Hardware Implementations of PQC ML-KEM Verification
quick_skim.abstract: As ML-KEM is adopted as a post-quantum cryptographic standard, resilience against physical side-channel attacks has become essential. Among the constituent steps, the decapsulation Fujisaki-Okamoto (FO) verification is particularly vulnerable to side-channel power and electromagnetic (EM) analysis. In this work, we focus on common FPGA-based implementations and examine their side-channel vulnerabilities, and compare them with those of microcontroller implementations. Three verification implementations, unprotected, hash-based (first-order), and higher-order masked, are evaluated for side-channel security on both a microcontroller and an FPGA. While FPGAs offer higher speed and parallelism, they often exhibit stronger side-channel leakage, especially in high bandwidth configurations. The higher-order masked designs still leak information about the underlying data due to hardware-level effects and data-dependent processing. Our experiments show that their parallelized processing on FPGAs introduces sufficient first-order leakage for full secret-key recovery. These results underscore the persistent challenge of securing PQC algorithms in performance-constrained and parallelized hardware environments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: evaluates side-channel protections for post-quantum crypto hardware, relevant to TEE security
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估后量子密码硬件的侧信道防护，涉及TEE安全基础
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估后量子密码硬件的侧信道防护，涉及TEE安全基础
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 评估后量子密码硬件的侧信道防护，涉及TEE安全基础
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes side-channel vulnerabilities in FPGA and microcontroller implementations of ML-KEM verification, comparing unprotected, masked, and hash-based protections. Findings inform secure hardware design for post-quantum systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文聚焦后量子密码标准ML-KEM在FPGA和微控制器上的物理侧信道安全性，评估了无保护、哈希掩蔽和高阶掩蔽三种实现。实验发现FPGA尽管速度更快但泄漏更强，且高阶掩蔽的成本效益比可能不利。该研究为TEE等安全硬件中密码原语的抗侧信道设计提供了实证指导。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文聚焦后量子密码标准ML-KEM在FPGA和微控制器上的物理侧信道安全性，评估了无保护、哈希掩蔽和高阶掩蔽三种实现。实验发现FPGA尽管速度更快但泄漏更强，且高阶掩蔽的成本效益比可能不利。该研究为TEE等安全硬件中密码原语的抗侧信道设计提供了实证指导。

quick_skim.title: DroidBreaker: Practical and Functional Problem-Space Attacks on Machine-Learning Android Malware Detectors
quick_skim.abstract: Adversarial APKs are Android applications modified in the problem space to evade machine-learning malware detectors. In this work, we first show that, despite claims, existing problem-space attacks remain largely impractical. Most techniques leverage software transplantation to inject entire benign modules, introducing many side-effect features and often causing build-time failures. Fine-grained methods that inject only a narrow subset of components exhibit limited effectiveness, while those that also use obfuscation rely on brittle bytecode rewriting, producing APKs that are syntactically valid but semantically unusable. Prior work further overestimates attack success rates by running smoke tests that only validate installation and basic execution, without assessing whether the modified APK still preserves its intended behavior. To overcome these limitations, we present DROIDBREAKER, a practical (build-safe) and functional (semantics-preserving) problem-space attack framework that provides: (i) query-efficient white- and black-box attacks by manipulating only the APK components most influential to the target model; (ii) a set of fine-grained, build-safe manipulations (including injection and obfuscation of API calls, app modules, permissions, and URLs) with minimal side effects; and (iii) a semantics-preserving functionality test that enforces runtime equivalence by comparing execution logs and API-level traces between the initial and the modified APK. Evaluated on a recent corpus of Android applications, DROIDBREAKER achieves high evasion rates with few queries and minimal side effects in both white-box and black-box settings, and drastically reduces detections by commercial malware scanners hosted on VirusTotal.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: adversarial malware generation against ML detectors, relevant to malware analysis and software security testing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对ML恶意软件检测器的对抗样本生成，涉及恶意软件分析与安全测试
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对ML恶意软件检测器的对抗样本生成，涉及恶意软件分析与安全测试
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对ML恶意软件检测器的对抗样本生成，涉及恶意软件分析与安全测试
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Reveals limitations of existing adversarial Android malware generation and proposes a practical problem-space attack to evaluate ML malware detectors, enhancing security testing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文指出现有针对安卓恶意软件检测器的对抗样本生成方法存在实用性不足的问题，提出DroidBreaker，一种更实用的问题空间攻击，通过精细化的软件移植与混淆技术，生成能逃避机器学习检测器且功能正常的APK，从而提升了对恶意软件检测系统的安全测试能力。

quick_skim.title: LIB-TRAP: Standard Cell Library Hardware Trojan Risk Assessment and Prevention
quick_skim.abstract: Vulnerabilities inherent to the fabless semiconductor manufacturing model have significantly increased the risk of malicious Hardware Trojan (HT) insertion, posing severe threats to hardware security. Several HT mitigation and detection strategies have been developed, and existing works explore the insertion of HTs in the space between standard cells in an integrated circuit. However, there is a lack of research into the vulnerabilities posed by the building blocks of most digital designs on the market today, the standard cells. This work investigates a novel threat model in which standard cells are considered untrusted. Our proposed threat model provides the design house with a tampered standard cell library. The intended netlist is synthesized and implemented using the tampered library. During fabrication, a nefarious foundry replaces the library's deactivated HT cells with activated counterparts. Using open-source and industry-standard Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools, existing standard cell libraries, Saed32nm and Sky130nm, are converted into malicious libraries capable of masking the presence of arbitrary HTs from IC designers. The malicious library is then applied and characterized in multiple standard benchmark designs. To demonstrate the efficacy and stealthiness of this standard cell-based attack vector, three benchmark circuits, an AES-128 encryption core, an Ethernet controller, and a WISHBONE DMA engine, were synthesized using both clean and Trojan-infected libraries across Synopsys 32nm and SkyWater 130nm technologies. Design-level features, including total cell count, total area, dynamic power consumption, and static power, were extracted from these synthesized circuits to serve as inputs for binary classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Hardware Trojan risk assessment in standard cell libraries, relevant to hardware security in system security

quick_skim.title: Your Space is My Zone: Demystifying the Security Risks of AI-Powered Applications on Pre-Trained Model Hubs
quick_skim.abstract: AI-powered Applications (AI-Apps), hosted on platforms such as Hugging Face, are democratizing access to pre-trained models through online inference and fine-tuning services. While lowering AI adoption barriers, these platforms introduce an unexplored attack surface, as AI-Apps are often developed by untrusted parties with weak isolation and misconfigured security settings. In this paper, we present the first systematic security analysis of AI-Apps across three leading platforms. To structure our investigation, we map the AI-App lifecycle to established risk taxonomies (e.g., OWASP), identifying five threat categories and ten attack vectors ranging from generic web flaws to high-impact architectural issues. Our analysis reveals critical failures including broken access control, insecure resource reuse, insufficient input validation, and sensitive data exposure. Notably, we uncover three novel architectural vulnerabilities inherent to platform design and demonstrate how traditional issues (e.g., world-readable logs) are uniquely amplified in this ecosystem. To assess real-world impact, we develop an analysis framework Insightor and apply it to over 970,000 public AI-Apps. Alarmingly, we find thousands of apps leaking credentials, hundreds containing input injection vulnerabilities that allow arbitrary code execution, and tens harboring embedded backdoors -- indicating active exploitation. We have responsibly disclosed all findings to the affected platforms and developers.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: systematic security analysis identifies attack vectors in AI-Apps
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 系统安全分析识别AI应用中的攻击向量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 系统安全分析识别AI应用中的攻击向量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 系统安全分析识别AI应用中的攻击向量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents the first systematic security analysis of AI-powered applications on platforms like Hugging Face, mapping the AI-App lifecycle to risk taxonomies and identifying five threat categories and ten attack vectors, revealing weak isolation and misconfigurations as key risks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文首次对Hugging Face等平台上的AI应用进行系统化安全分析，将AI应用生命周期映射到风险分类体系（如OWASP），识别出五类威胁和十种攻击向量。分析揭示了不可信开发者导致的隔离薄弱和安全配置错误等关键风险，为保障AI应用生态的安全提供了方法论基础。

quick_skim.title: HTTP REST API Structure Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) are essential in software development, enabling web services, mobile apps, and microservices. However, their widespread use introduces significant security risks, highlighting the importance of API security. This paper presents HTTP REST API Learning (HRAL), a novel unsupervised anomaly detection approach that models the structure and behavior of API endpoints directly from network traffic, without relying on predefined rules or documentation. HRAL enables robust detection of malicious activity by understanding how APIs behave and flagging deviations as potential threats. We evaluate HRAL across varying levels of OpenAPI documentation detail and compare it with existing techniques. HRAL achieves strong performance, with an average recall of 82.07% and an F1-score of 87.24%, significantly outperforming alternatives when API documentation is limited. Moreover, our results approach the effectiveness of full API document definitions. When combined with signature-based rules such as the OWASP ModSecurity CRS, our system achieves 100% detection. These results highlight HRAL's effectiveness in real-world, partially documented API environments and its potential as a foundational layer for modern API security solutions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: unsupervised anomaly detection on API traffic, a dynamic analysis technique for runtime security monitoring
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 对API流量的无监督异常检测，一种运行时安全监控的动态分析技术
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 对API流量的无监督异常检测，一种运行时安全监控的动态分析技术
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 对API流量的无监督异常检测，一种运行时安全监控的动态分析技术
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes HRAL, an unsupervised learning method that automatically models API structure from network traffic to detect malicious activities, achieving 82% recall without documentation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出HTTP REST API结构学习(HRAL)方法，一种无监督异常检测技术，通过从网络流量中自动学习API端点的结构与行为模式，无需预定义规则或文档，即可有效检测恶意活动。实验表明HRAL在不同OpenAPI文档细节程度下平均召回率达82.07%，为API安全提供了一种灵活的动态分析方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出HTTP REST API结构学习(HRAL)方法，一种无监督异常检测技术，通过从网络流量中自动学习API端点的结构与行为模式，无需预定义规则或文档，即可有效检测恶意活动。实验表明HRAL在不同OpenAPI文档细节程度下平均召回率达82.07%，为API安全提供了一种灵活的动态分析方案。

quick_skim.title: Chameleon: Recovering Cyber-Physical Systems from Memory Corruption Attacks via ML Surrogates
quick_skim.abstract: Cyber-physical systems (CPSs) are increasingly deployed in every aspect of our lives and can be compromised through memory corruption vulnerabilities, allowing attackers to hijack the control flow and take over the system. Existing techniques mostly focus on detecting such attacks but respond by terminating or halting execution upon attack detection, which is not acceptable in CPSs used in safety-critical tasks, as interrupted tasks can have catastrophic consequences. Other techniques replace compromised CPS components with simplified defaults that degrade system behavior, or reboot the system upon attack detection.   We propose Chameleon, a novel framework for automatically recovering CPSs from memory corruption attacks using machine learning (ML)-based surrogates trained at compartment granularity that nearly replicate their original compartments' behavior but do not have the same memory corruption vulnerabilities. Upon attack detection, Chameleon replaces the compromised compartment with its trained surrogate. We implemented Chameleon using the LLVM compiler and evaluated its efficiency and effectiveness on seven different robotic vehicles (RVs), including simulated and real ones. We found that Chameleon can generate surrogates that closely approximate the original compartments (with an average R$^2$=0.96), successfully recover the system despite real-world memory corruption attacks unlike prior approaches, and complete their tasks while incurring low performance and memory overhead.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes ML-based recovery from memory corruption attacks to prevent system failure in safety-critical CPS.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于ML的内存损坏攻击恢复方法，防止安全关键CPS系统失效。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出基于ML的内存损坏攻击恢复方法，防止安全关键CPS系统失效。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出基于ML的内存损坏攻击恢复方法，防止安全关键CPS系统失效。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Addresses memory corruption attacks in CPS by introducing Chameleon, a framework that uses ML surrogates to automatically recover compromised systems, avoiding termination or degraded behavior. It demonstrates safe recovery in safety-critical tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对网络物理系统面临的内存损坏攻击，现有方法在检测后常终止或降级运行，对安全关键任务不可接受。本文提出Chameleon框架，利用机器学习代理在攻击检测后自动恢复系统，避免中断和降级。实验表明该方法能在安全关键任务中实现安全恢复，为CPS安全提供新的防御思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对网络物理系统面临的内存损坏攻击，现有方法在检测后常终止或降级运行，对安全关键任务不可接受。本文提出Chameleon框架，利用机器学习代理在攻击检测后自动恢复系统，避免中断和降级。实验表明该方法能在安全关键任务中实现安全恢复，为CPS安全提供新的防御思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Advanced techniques for software vulnerability detection and exploitation prevention
